name: Everett Barksdale
abstract: Everett Barksdale (April 28, 1910 – January 29, 1986) was an American jazz guitarist and session musician.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz
Midnight Performance: Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: It was — the drummer was Osie Johnson and the bass player was Wendell Marshall. I played piano. A guy by the name of Everett Barksdale played guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: an especially good friend of mine in those days... was Everett Barksdale

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Everett Barksdale was one of the people who was on the gig. I was the contractor at the time, and Barksdale and I – after a while, there were several black musicians involved in jingles.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, but you really set those rhythm patterns, because Ernie Hays and Everett Barksdale. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, and Carl Lynch.

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: I was away. Slam got with him for a while with Everett Barksdale, but it just didn't go.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: You mentioned him before as part of Herman Chittison Trio. Right. And Everett
